verifiedBy: Codex via gh CLI and authenticated artifact API download
notes: Success artifact governance-evidence-28304098315 (artifact ID 7928840112) and controlled-failure artifact governance-evidence-28306149811 (artifact ID 7929421387) were downloaded into isolated temporary directories and independently verified.

The validated implementation commit advanced to protected-master merge commit 2704049d7e826975d956611b194214dd79ea3686 because PR #5 merged executable evidence-validator semantics, shared governance-validation behavior, and regression tests.

The success artifact ZIP SHA-256 was independently computed as 8cb3dec5db93e1834c38b291ee4445f9c8c69f4954e3152a6c1f296da8d205dd; the GitHub-reported digest was sha256:8cb3dec5db93e1834c38b291ee4445f9c8c69f4954e3152a6c1f296da8d205dd.

The controlled-failure artifact ZIP SHA-256 was independently computed as b50936c7c1575af9cce201a9a0e36a46dfc1ce30752482c21d1ca008ae5b0bd2; the GitHub-reported digest was sha256:b50936c7c1575af9cce201a9a0e36a46dfc1ce30752482c21d1ca008ae5b0bd2.

The controlled-failure path deliberately injected a failed mandatory Markdown outcome after successful Markdown report generation. Evidence generation, completion-evidence validation, and artifact upload completed successfully; final mandatory enforcement rejected the synthetic failed mandatory result, producing the expected overall workflow failure.
Later documentation and evidence metadata commits may record this verification without changing the immutable release target model.
Artifact evidence uses evidenceCommitSha null because workflow artifacts are immutable GitHub Actions downloads and are not committed back to the validated source commit.
Historical success run 28293025156 and controlled-failure run 28297679210 remain audit evidence for the earlier PR #3 release target 072df3c372d431e3ac5fd0e4569b55f93555ce95.
